title: Fix Reflected XSS in Migration Scorecard URL Fields
description:
**Penetration Test Finding — Medium Severity**

URL fields in the Migration Scorecard Record were vulnerable to Reflected XSS. Users with migration editing permissions could enter `javascript:alert(document.domain)//` in URL fields. When any user clicked the link, the XSS payload executed.

## Affected Endpoint

`/dashboard?view=migration`

## Risk

Medium — requires user interaction (clicking the link) for execution. Attacker could execute arbitrary JavaScript and exfiltrate CSRF tokens.

## Fix

URL validation rule implemented so all links must be proper URLs. Invalid URLs trigger a server-side validation error displayed below each affected field.
acceptance_criteria: URL fields validate that values are well-formed URLs
javascript: protocol and other non-HTTP schemes are rejected
Server returns validation errors for invalid URL values displayed below each field
Legitimate URLs are accepted and rendered as clickable links
ux_changes: Validation error messages now display below URL fields when invalid URLs are entered.
middleware_changes: Server-side URL validation rule added to migration scorecard update controller. Non-HTTP URLs are rejected with descriptive error responses.
implementation_notes: Unlike the Stored XSS fixes which use HTML entity encoding, this fix validates URL format to ensure only proper http/https URLs are accepted.
comment_summary: Developer implemented URL validation on staging. All links must be proper URLs; invalid entries show server-side validation errors below each field.
